content.full_text:
GUS J. SKROPOS
Councilman
City of Ontario
P.O. Box 1607
Ontario, CA 91762
ID # 902-084

January 17, 1992


Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812-0807


Dear Mr. Greenwood:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of
January 16, 1992.

Yesterday, I indicated to you that I currently have an
active campaign committee and bank account which I opened
and established prior to my election to the Ontario City
Council in November of 1990. I told you that since the
election I have done some fundraising to extinguish a
campaign debt and raise some funds for expenses during my
term of office. You, as well as others in your organization, indicated that this was no problem utilizing the
existing committee.

I inquired about the mechanism I should use to raise funds
for my re-election campaign in 1994. Specifically, I wanted
to know if I should open a new bank account and committee,
or, amend the existing committee and account via forms 501
and 502. You inquired and told me that I should use the
existing organization and account and file the amended forms
indicating the 1994 election date. You said that this would
be appropriate if I was not carrying a debt from my last
campaign.

Per your advice, I am enclosing the amended forms 501 and
502 you requested.

I would like to express my appreciation for your assistance
in clearing up my confusion on this matter.

Sincerely,

Gus J. Skropos


Not Printed at Government Expense

sections.question_synthetic: Can an elected official use an existing campaign committee and bank account for a re-election campaign in 1994, or should a new committee and account be opened?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence from a city council member confirming advice already given by telephone. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The document appears to be a follow-up letter enclosing amended forms rather than a formal FPPC advice letter. No clear question and answer structure visible.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can an elected official use an existing campaign committee and bank account for a re-election campaign in 1994, or should a new committee and account be opened?

embedding.summary: Gus Skropos writes to confirm a telephone conversation with FPPC staff regarding whether to use his existing campaign committee and bank account for his 1994 re-election campaign. He indicates that FPPC advised him to amend the existing committee and account using forms 501 and 502, provided he carries no campaign debt from the previous election.
